title: Theory about Kaworus intentions
wikitext:
[]: [SPOILERS]Theory about Kaworu's intentions.             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed May 22, 2013 4:30 pm Here goes a theory that I came upon after analyzing Kaworu's behavior thorough 3.33 : what if Kaworu was actually loyal to SEELE to the end? What if Kaworu was working to bring forth Instrumentality? Bear with me, here come the explanation of how I came to this theory : Firstly, there is something I always tough odd during the fight between Asuka and Shinji : why Kaworu never tried to help Shinji in trying to reason Asuka? I understand that he was deep in thoughts because he felt something was wrong... but right in front of him there was a German fury hellbent on skewering them with a giant glaive, and his partner had all the difficulties on the world to keep her at bay because he didn't wanted to harm her, and their magical drones were being destroyed one behind the other! Basically during that battle Shinji wanted to make understand to Asuka that he didn't wanted to destroy the world with the spears, but to actually save it, and her answers where interesting : Firstly when Shinji tells her that he's indeed piloting and that will change the world by doing it, she responds : 01:09:15 {Asuka} If that's how it is, don't pilot!

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed May 22, 2013 4:57 pm About the "catalyst" thing... The original word, 原因 (gen'in), actually means "cause/origin/source". Guess the translation needs to be fixed again...

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Wed May 22, 2013 5:13 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed May 22, 2013 5:39 pm Now as I said, take care : I really think that Kaworu genuinely want Shinji's happiness, he was always talking about making him happy, even when he was alone. The thing is that we don't know "what" Kaworu meant by "his happiness". Since he was talking about it even before the cataclysmic event that turned Earth into the hellish world that it is in 3.0, he was obviously thinking of something else than fixing the world with the two spears, and I really doubt that he foresaw these apocalyptic events, if that was the case bringing happiness to Shinji would have simply been to make sure that these mysterious events leading to 3I don't happens. That's why I think that this time, maybe Kaworu's goals could coincide with SEELE's : in NGE Kaworu finally went against SEELE because he though that unifying the Lilin in a single conscience would destroy everything their individuality accomplished(their culture, music, technology, philosophy...), but here in Rebuild they want to make evolve mankind into a new specie blessed with a Fruit of Life, and Kaworu never said that he though that it wasn't good for them, quite the contrary with his speech about how mankind's only way to evolve is to "brought the rite of artificial evolution upon themselves" and that it "has been programmed since time immemorial", it looks like he think that it's mankind destiny. Beside being with both fruits should be people with who he could relate more easily, since in a way he's the same : an Angel(FoL) in an human body(FoK), that would explain his recurrent discourses about learning new thing and repeating them : 00:41:58 {Kaworu} If you want to live well, you have to change and try new things.

[]: Re: [SPOILERS]Theory about Kaworu's intentions.             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed May 22, 2013 5:39 pm ElMariachi wrote: Here goes a theory that I came upon after analyzing Kaworu's behavior thorough 3.33 : what if Kaworu was actually loyal to SEELE to the end? What if Kaworu was working to bring forth Instrumentality? .... If Kaworu though that Instrumentality indeed was mankind's destiny, and that all that SEELE wanted this time was the "peaceful purification of all souls" brought by Human Instrumentality, why would SEELE's goal be incompatible with Kaworu's objective to make Shinji happy? After all we don't know what he meant by "making Shinji happy", and he was talking about that even before 3.0, where making him happy would simply be letting him fix the world. So what if Kaworu's goal was to make Shinji the center of Instrumentality so that he could temporarily become a god and shape a world to fit to his own definition of happiness. Shinji would create his own happiness, SEELE would have their purification of the soul they wanted so much, and everyone is contented!

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed May 22, 2013 5:53 pm I think that like in NGE, Instrumentality includes an Impact to work. I'm sure that Kaworu knew that the 12th Angel is alive, and fully intended to awaken EVA-13 with it to open the Chamber of Guf, and from here start SEELE's Instrumentality thanks the the two different spears. Remember that once everything is over Fuyutsuki said that "nearly everything went as Seele had intended", that means that EVA-13 awakening and Fourth Impact was part of SEELEs plan. Surely if the awakening happens with the two same spears, that must make SEELE's version of Instrumentality unable to happen, that's why Kaworu tried to stop Shinji from pulling them. But Gendo had already "prepared" the field by messing so much with his son's sanity that he still carried on and pulled the spears for the off chance that it would restore the world's ecosystem, or rewind the time, or whatever he tought would happen, which wouldn't have happened anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed May 22, 2013 6:03 pm Actually, Gendo's words appear to imply that Eva-13's awakening wasn't part of Seele's plan. I think Eva-13 was simply meant to fetch the two spears and use them, not serve as a medium for the creation of the 13th angel. We still haven't considered how Eva-13 being an awakened "Adam" effects or affects everyone's plans.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed May 22, 2013 6:10 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Actually, Gendo's words appear to imply that Eva-13's awakening wasn't part of Seele's plan. I think Eva-13 was simply meant to fetch the two spears and use them, not serve as a medium for the creation of the 13th angel. We still haven't considered how Eva-13 being an awakened "Adam" effects or affects everyone's plans.

[]:             Quote Post by Rei IV » Wed May 22, 2013 6:10 pm Jeez, for some reason, I find this scenario totally plausible. Kaworu isn't exactly the bad guy, per say, but certainly played a role in the destruction of Shinji's remaining shred of sanity.....Just like in NGE.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Wed May 22, 2013 6:39 pm This all does make a lot of sense but I still can't get rid of my irrational nagging feelings about Kaworu. I can't even decide whether it would be more interesting or more boring if it turned out that Kaworu had his own master plan (that doesn't involve Shinji's actual happiness in any way) and simply maniputed everyone from Seele to Shinji to his own ends. Seele, humanity, blessed with one Fruit wants to claim the other for themselves. Why couldn't the reverse be just as true? Kaworu seems to have tasted both Fruits, what if he liked it? The events of Q could be interpreted as him trying to get an equal footing with the Lilim by getting rid of his own Lilim form and claiming that of an awakened Eva: both fruits and keys to the Doors of Guf. If the final battle turns out to be children of each Fruit fighting to claim both while wiping out the other......yeah again I don't really know whether that's more interesting or not.... I realize the case presented earlier in the thread is much stronger and my own might even be thematically un-Eva-ish, but this is a pretty constant nagging for me.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Wed May 22, 2013 10:16 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: This all does make a lot of sense but I still can't get rid of my irrational nagging feelings about Kaworu.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Thu May 23, 2013 12:17 am TMBounty_Hunter wrote: If the final battle turns out to be children of each Fruit fighting to claim both while wiping out the other......yeah again I don't really know whether that's more interesting or not.... I realize the case presented earlier in the thread is much stronger and my own might even be thematically un-Eva-ish, but this is a pretty constant nagging for me.

[]:             Quote Post by jackysee » Thu May 23, 2013 3:18 am 01:01:32 {Kaworu} That which was changed by Eva can be restored by Eva in turn.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu May 23, 2013 3:55 am @ TMBounty_Hunter & Shinoyami65 : I still think that Kaworu's desire for Shinji's happiness are genuine, we saw him still talking about how this time Shinji will be happy even when he's completely alone : at the end of 1.0, the very last line of the movie when he's on the Moon looking at the Earth, and at the end of 2.0 when he's in the cockpit of Mark.06. If his intention to bring Shinji's happiness where a façade, he wouldn't be talking about it even when alone. Besides I don't really think that SEELE gave two shit about why he would want to launch Instrumentality, as long as it's launched, so that's not a façade he put in front of SEELE either. Besides, Kaworu seems to really enjoy having a FoK(or pseudo-one) that let him learn new things, if his objective was simply to wipe out the rest of Lilith based life, then that would mean populating Earth with creature unable to learn or change, that would be utterly boring for someone like Kaworu. But bringing ADAM-based and Lilith-based lifeforms on an equal foot, that's more like something Kaworu would like to achieve. And on a meta-level, given the shitton of marketing and artwork playing with Kaworu's affection for Shinji(with touches of Ho-Yay added for the flavour), revealing that he's actually an evil manipulator on the same level that Gendo would utterly destroy any hype for the character. And not talking about the fact that HyperShinshan will probably commit suicide! jackysee wrote: 01:01:32 {Kaworu} That which was changed by Eva can be restored by Eva in turn.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Thu May 23, 2013 6:38 am Very interesting theory, and one that I have somewhat skirted in the "Hidden Agendas" thread. I have a few things to add. 1. Perhaps Kaworu never responded to Asuka because he never wanted anyone outside of NERV or SEELE to know of his existence. I don't recall Asuka, Mari, Misato or Ritsuko ever acknowledging that Unit-13 had a second pilot, let alone Kaworu's existence. (Although Mari appears to grasp what is going on after the DSS Choker kills Kaworu, and she realizes Shinji is still alive and must be ejected from Unit-13.) 2. In the end, Kaworu did betray SEELE. This was when he halted Fourth Impact for Shinji's sake. (Please disregard the timestamps.) Dialogue: 0,1:34:09.35,1:34:11.75,Default,,0,0,0,,{Shinji} This is all because of me... Dialogue: 0,1:34:13.43,1:34:15.57,Default,,0,0,0,,{Shinji} I... Dialogue: 0,1:34:18.98,1:34:20.80,Default,,0,0,0,,{Kaworu} It's not your fault. Dialogue: 0,1:34:22.86,1:34:26.86,Default,,0,0,0,,{Kaworu} It's because I became the Thirteenth Angel. Dialogue: 0,1:34:26.86,1:34:28.86,Default,,0,0,0,,{Kaworu} I'm the trigger. Dialogue: 0,1:34:28.86,1:34:31.86,Default,,0,0,0,,{Shinji} What do I do? Dialogue: 0,1:34:31.86,1:34:34.38,Default,,0,0,0,,{Shinji} Hey, what are we going to do? Dialogue: 0,1:34:38.86,1:34:41.86,Default,,0,0,0,,{Shinji} Kaworu-kun, Dialogue: 0,1:34:41.86,1:34:44.86,Default,,0,0,0,,{Shinji} tell me what the right thing would be. Dialogue: 0,1:34:44.86,1:34:50.86,Default,,0,0,0,,{Kaworu} Even if souls disappear, hopes and curses will remain in this world. Dialogue: 0,1:34:50.86,1:34:55.86,Default,,0,0,0,,{Kaworu} Intent is imparted to the world as information and goes on to change. Dialogue: 0,1:34:55.86,1:34:58.70,Default,,0,0,0,,{Kaworu} In time, even one's very self is being rewritten. Dialogue: 0,1:35:00.62,1:35:05.40,Default,,0,0,0,,{Kaworu} I'm sorry. This isn't the happiness you wished for. Dialogue: 0,1:35:06.86,1:35:09.14,Default,,0,0,0,,{Kaworu} I will close the Door of Guf. Dialogue: 0,1:35:09.22,1:35:12.86,Default,,0,0,0,,{Kaworu} You don't need to worry about it, Shinji-kun. Dialogue: 0,1:35:12.86,1:35:15.86,Default,,0,0,0,,{Shinji} Kaworu-kun... Dialogue: 0,1:35:15.86,1:35:17.94,Default,,0,0,0,,{Shinji} What are you saying, Kaworu-kun? Dialogue: 0,1:35:17.96,1:35:19.60,Default,,0,0,0,,{Shinji} I don't get any of it!

[]:             Quote Post by xdiesp » Thu May 23, 2013 7:23 am I wonder what neophytes think of rebuilt Kaworu: without the series to back him up, he's really a quitter space pianist. Not so cool if you don't know him from before. Quite a long fall from the number one spot as Japanese Jesus.

[]:             Quote Post by Charsi » Thu May 23, 2013 7:28 am Jornophelanthas wrote: 2. In the end, Kaworu did betray SEELE. This was when he halted Fourth Impact for Shinji's sake. (Please disregard the timestamps.)

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu May 23, 2013 7:35 am @ Jornophelanthas : 1. I doubt that WILLE don't know about Kaworu's existence : this is the guy who descended from the sky and stopped N3I with a never seen before weapon and Evangelion, I really doubt that after that he just flew back to the Moon like the masked guy in Sailor Moon! Besides if the trailer in 2.0 actually show events of the time skip, then stayed at NERV(we see him wearing a NERV uniform during the meeting with the "four children chosen by fate") 2. The fact that Kaworu participated in the closing of the Door of Guf don't mean that he betrayed SEELE, since this version of 4I with two Longinus spears isn't what SEELE had planed to begin with. Kaworu was very adamant about the fact that they needed a Cassius and a Longinus type, and when explaining his plan to Shinji before, he said that the two lances were vital for NERV(meaning SEELE) to launch Instrumentality. That's why he tried to stop Shinji for pulling out the spears : from the moment he noticed that the spears were the same, he understood that his and SEELE's version of instrumentality was doomed to failure. Had Shinji listened to Kaworu, he probably wouldn't had asked him to surrender to WILLE, but to climb back to neo-NERV HQ to interrogate Gendo.(they probably would have to break Asuka's teeth before, but something tells me that if Kaworu joins Shinji in piloting, that would have ended in a curb-stomb battle, even if Shinji refuses to fight against her) 3. That's a possibility, after al the laws of reality seems to apply more loosely to him than for others. Still if that's true, the fact he seems to continuously fail should be an hint that his method isn't the correct one.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Thu May 23, 2013 8:49 am But if Kaworu royally fucked up Seele's plans, why would Fuyutsuki say: 01:27:35 {Fuyutsuki} Nearly everything went as Seele had intended. 01:27:38 {Gendo} Even so, we eliminated Seele's boy and guided Eva-13 to its Awakening.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Thu May 23, 2013 9:02 am Charsi wrote: Except that Seele had a backup. The dual entry system meant the impact event would continue until BOTH pilots were removed or killed. They (both Seele and NERV) already accounted for the possibility of a DSS choker. The Door of Guf did not close when Kaworu speared Eva-13, it didn't close when his head was blown off, it ONLY closed after Mari force-ejected Shinji's plug, removing the last remaining pilot from the Evangelion. What seems likely to me is that Seele expected Shinji to be wearing the DSS choker. NERV was betting on Kaworu taking the choker from Shinji. So each expected a different pilot to die in the process, thereby quashing the other's plans (no doubt Shinji is absolutely essential to whatever Gendo has planned).

Dialogue: 0,1:34:31.86,1:34:34.38,Default,,0,0,0,,{Shinji} Hey, what are we going to do? Dialogue: 0,1:34:38.86,1:34:41.86,Default,,0,0,0,,{Shinji} Kaworu-kun, Dialogue: 0,1:34:41.86,1:34:44.86,Default,,0,0,0,,{Shinji} tell me what the right thing would be. Dialogue: 0,1:34:44.86,1:34:50.86,Default,,0,0,0,,{Kaworu} Even if souls disappear, hopes and curses will remain in this world. Dialogue: 0,1:34:50.86,1:34:55.86,Default,,0,0,0,,{Kaworu} Intent is imparted to the world as information and goes on to change. Dialogue: 0,1:34:55.86,1:34:58.70,Default,,0,0,0,,{Kaworu} In time, even one's very self is being rewritten. Dialogue: 0,1:35:00.62,1:35:05.40,Default,,0,0,0,,{Kaworu} I'm sorry. This isn't the happiness you wished for. Dialogue: 0,1:35:06.86,1:35:09.14,Default,,0,0,0,,{Kaworu} I will close the Door of Guf. Dialogue: 0,1:35:09.22,1:35:12.86,Default,,0,

Article: Theory about Kaworus intentions
.14,Default,,0,0,0,,{Kaworu} I will close the Door of Guf. Dialogue: 0,1:35:09.22,1:35:12.86,Default,,0,0,0,,{Kaworu} You don't need to worry about it, Shinji-kun. Dialogue: 0,1:35:12.86,1:35:15.86,Default,,0,0,0,,{Shinji} Kaworu-kun... Dialogue: 0,1:35:15.86,1:35:17.94,Default,,0,0,0,,{Shinji} What are you saying, Kaworu-kun? Dialogue: 0,1:35:17.96,1:35:19.60,Default,,0,0,0,,{Shinji} I don't get any of it!

Article: Theory about Kaworus intentions
 NERV(we see him wearing a NERV uniform during the meeting with the "four children chosen by fate") 2. The fact that Kaworu participated in the closing of the Door of Guf don't mean that he betrayed SEELE, since this version of 4I with two Longinus spears isn't what SEELE had planed to begin with. Kaworu was very adamant about the fact that they needed a Cassius and a Longinus type, and when explaining his plan to Shinji before, he said that the two lances were vital for NERV(meaning SEELE) to launch Instrumentality. That's why he tried to stop Shinji for pulling out the spears : from the moment he noticed that the spears were the same, he understood that his and SEELE's version of instrumentality was doomed to failure. Had Shinji listened to Kaworu, he probably wouldn't had asked him to surrender to WILLE, but to climb back to neo-NERV HQ to interrogate Gendo.(they probably would have to break Asuka's teeth before, but something tells me that if Kaworu joins Shinji in piloting, that would have ended in a curb-stomb battle, even if Shinji refuses to fight against her) 3. That's a possibility, after al the laws of reality seems to apply more loosely to him than for others. Still if that's true, the fact he seems to continuously fail should be an hint that his method isn't the correct one.

The Door of Guf did not close when Kaworu speared Eva-13, it didn't close when his head was blown

Article: Theory about Kaworus intentions
 or killed. They (both Seele and NERV) already accounted for the possibility of a DSS choker. The Door of Guf did not close when Kaworu speared Eva-13, it didn't close when his head was blown off, it ONLY closed after Mari force-ejected Shinji's plug, removing the last remaining pilot from the Evangelion. What seems likely to me is that Seele expected Shinji to be wearing the DSS choker. NERV was betting on Kaworu taking the choker from Shinji. So each expected a different pilot to die in the process, thereby quashing the other's plans (no doubt Shinji is absolutely essential to whatever Gendo has planned).
